what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory ledger entry that records an image attachment for the design-assets channel, linking it to a specific image asset by URI and recording when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter only; no body content
Target is image://design-assets/diagrams/Visual Assets - Dev Space - Frame 8
standouts: Target uses an image:// URI pointing to a Figma-style frame name
Entry is purely metadata with no explanatory text
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a stable att-* id
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone
Follows a minimal, file-per-attachment ledger pattern
concerns: No title, caption, or alt text for the image
No indication of who added the attachment or why
Target URI is opaque and not resolvable from this file alone
notes: A bare attachment ledger record that ties the design-assets channel to a specific diagram image via a dated metadata entry.
questions: What is 'Visual Assets - Dev Space - Frame 8' and what does it depict?
Who added this attachment and in what channel message context?
Is the image:// URI resolvable elsewhere in the system?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a channel attachment, and the file does exactly that with minimal metadata.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a persistent, referenceable record of a design image so the channel memory system can track which visual assets belong to the design-assets channel.
novelty: The target URI embeds a Figma-style frame name ('Frame 8') within an image:// scheme, making the asset reference human-readable but tool-specific.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel and a specific image asset, which a fused system could use to surface design artifacts alongside channel context.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment record for the design-assets channel that references an image asset at image://design-assets/diagrams/Visual Assets - Dev Space - Frame 8 and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a design image attachment so it can be recalled as part of the design-assets channel's memory.
